commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review is needed because the PR lacks contributor real behavior proof and the remaining blocker is a security-sensitive durable message contract change.; Needs attention: The patch introduces a trusted persisted bareBody path that can skip existing inbound system-marker neutralization.; Review finding: [P1] Sanitize BareBody before trusting it — src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-context.ts:88 (sha=fa8c522103576cb47a624aa41ec12338d400a8cd)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds trusted inbound-decoration metadata for decorated user turns and updates transcript persistence, Control UI display/extraction, memory session export, and historical LLM replay to prefer a trusted bare body over sentinel parsing.

PR surface: Source +132, Tests +105. Total +237 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows a sentinel-shaped user message follows the same text-strip path on current main, and the PR head adds a trusted `BareBody` path that can be traced through persistence, UI, memory, and replay.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the PR lacks contributor real behavior proof and the remaining blocker is a security-sensitive durable message contract change.

target.title: fix #95279: Provide a trusted inbound-decoration contract so consumers can strip/dedup without forgeable text heuristics
target.body:
Fixes #95279

## Summary

- **What did NOT change:** This does not remove legacy sentinel stripping for already-persisted rows, does not migrate transcript files, does not change active-turn model prompt construction, and does not add unrelated provider/channel routing behavior.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Users who paste or type literal metadata-looking text should not lose that text from the control UI, memory session corpus, or model historical replay when OpenClaw has a trusted bare body for the inbound turn.
- Behavior or issue addressed: user-authored text that looks like OpenClaw inbound metadata could be stripped from UI/history/memory because consumers parsed forgeable sentinel text from `content`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/sessions/user-turn-transcript.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.llm-boundary.test.ts` and `git diff --check`.
- **Observed result after fix:** The targeted regression tests completed successfully with no test failures, and `git diff --check` completed with no whitespace errors.
- **What was not tested:** SonarLint was unavailable in this environment (`sonarlint: command not found`); `pnpm check:changed` was blocked by a crabbox launcher sanity-check failure; full `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --incremental false` hit Node heap out-of-memory even with `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192`.
- Real environment tested: local Windows/Git Bash worktree using the repository Vitest runner.
- Evidence after fix: targeted regression tests pass for transcript persistence, UI display, UI text extraction, memory-host session export, and LLM historical replay with trusted `bareBody`.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/sessions/user-turn-transcript.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.test.ts`, `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts`, and `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.llm-boundary.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** Each consumer test covers a decorated inbound turn whose trusted bare body contains sentinel-shaped user text such as `Sender (untrusted metadata):` plus fenced JSON; the expected behavior is that the trusted bare body is preserved literally instead of being parsed and stripped as metadata.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** It locks the shared trusted-contract behavior at the persistence, UI, memory, and LLM replay boundaries without removing the legacy sentinel fallback or asserting unrelated prompt-construction details.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** `buildInboundUserContextPrefix(...)` creates inbound metadata as plaintext blocks for model-facing prompts, and consumers such as UI history normalization, memory-host session export, and LLM historical replay had no trusted way to distinguish host-added decoration from user-authored text in the same `content` channel.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The source-of-truth decision is no longer derived from forgeable `content` text. Consumers now use the shared trusted resolver and only accept `bareBody` when the message/context is explicitly marked `inboundDecorated: true` / `InboundDecorated: true`, so sentinel parsing remains only a legacy compatibility path.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix for a public/data-format contract bug.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High. The change is additive, keeps legacy fallback behavior, and has targeted regression coverage for the affected consumer paths.
- **Patch quality notes:** Dynamic `as unknown` casts stay at existing message-union boundaries where `AgentMessage` can carry additive metadata; guard/default returns preserve existing fallback behavior; the multi-file diff follows the shared consumer contract boundary and is not a downstream masking workaround.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The canonical contract is the inbound user-turn/message metadata (`inboundDecorated` plus `bareBody`, mirrored as `InboundDecorated` plus `BareBody` on inbound context). Display/history/memory consumers use that trusted contract when present and only parse text sentinels for unmarked legacy content.

## What Problem This Solves

Inbound context decoration is host-generated, but the decorated copy can live as text inside user-controlled user message content. Several consumers stripped that decoration by matching forgeable sentinel strings such as `Sender (untrusted metadata):` and fenced JSON. If a user typed the same shape intentionally, downstream display/history/memory paths could drop real user text.

This PR gives consumers a trusted, out-of-band contract for decorated inbound turns: a message/context marker that says the content may be decorated, plus the host-authored bare body to render/replay/index.

## Source-of-truth boundary

The canonical contract is the inbound user-turn/message metadata:

- runtime/persisted user messages: `inboundDecorated: true` plus `bareBody`
- inbound context: `InboundDecorated` plus `BareBody`

The model-facing prompt may still carry inbound context for the active turn. Display/history/memory consumers should use the trusted bare body when present, and only parse text sentinels for unmarked legacy content.

## Implementation summary

- Add optional trusted inbound fields:
  - `inboundDecorated: true` / `bareBody` on persisted/runtime user messages.
  - `InboundDecorated` / `BareBody` on inbound template context.
- Persist the trusted bare body on decorated user turns, while respecting `before_message_write` hooks as the redaction boundary: if a hook replaces content without returning trusted fields, the original `bareBody` / `inboundDecorated` is dropped.
- Update UI display, UI text extraction/export/search, memory-host session indexing, and LLM historical replay to prefer the trusted bare body before falling back to legacy sentinel stripping.
- Keep existing `stripInboundMetadata(...)` behavior for old transcripts that do not carry the trusted fields.

## Scope boundary

In scope: contract/type plumbing for inbound user turns, transcript persistence/runtime merge preservation, UI display normalization, memory-host session export/indexing, LLM historical replay normalization, and regression tests for those paths.

Out of scope: removing legacy sentinel stripping for already-persisted rows, changing active-turn model prompt construction, migrating existing transcript files, or doing a broad plugin SDK redesign beyond optional trusted fields.

## Related PR scan

The daily-fix prework found related PR #94419 as advisory background only. This PR does not depend on that work and keeps the fix scoped to the trusted inbound bare-body contract requested by #95279.

## Review findings addressed

- RF-001 / RF-004 / RF-008 — fixed in `src/sessions/user-turn-transcript.ts`: `preparePersistedUserTurnMessageForTranscriptWrite(...)` now preserves the original idempotency key only. If `before_message_write` replaces/redacts a user message and does not return trusted inbound fields itself, the original `bareBody` and `inboundDecorated` are not reattached. Regression coverage: `src/sessions/user-turn-transcript.test.ts` extends the existing redaction/idempotency test with a decorated secret prompt and asserts the persisted redacted row has no original trusted bare-body fields.
- RF-005 / RF-009 — fixed in `ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.ts`: `extractText(...)` / `extractTextCached(...)` now use the shared `resolveTrustedInboundBareBody(...)` path for trusted decorated user messages before legacy sentinel stripping. Regression coverage: `ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.test.ts` verifies sentinel-shaped user-authored text is preserved for export/search/text-extraction consumers.
- RF-006 — addressed by keeping one shared resolver and targeted coverage across transcript persistence, UI render, UI extraction, memory-host session indexing, and LLM historical replay. The durable fields are additive and old transcripts without `inboundDecorated: true` continue through the legacy stripping path.
- RF-002 / RF-003 / RF-007 — proof gap disclosed: this environment does not have the external/live Mantis or full Control UI proof path available. Completed local proof is the targeted regression run listed below plus `git diff --check`; SonarLint, changed-check, and full TypeScript checks are explicitly listed as unavailable/blocked rather than claimed.

## Verification

Passed locally:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/sessions/user-turn-transcript.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.llm-boundary.test.ts
```

```text
git diff --check
```

## Merge risk

- Risk labels considered: `message-delivery`, `security-boundary`, `session-state`.
- Risk explanation: This changes display/replay/indexing behavior for messages that explicitly opt into the trusted decorated contract. The security-boundary risk is that consumers now trust `bareBody`; it is gated by `inboundDecorated === true` and only populated by host-side preparation/persistence paths. Session-state risk is additive because existing transcripts without the marker keep the old sentinel fallback.
- Why acceptable: The contract is optional and backward-compatible. It narrows unsafe behavior by making trusted fields authoritative only when present, while retaining legacy stripping for old rows. The tests cover the paths most likely to drop user-authored content.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
